title: Acilepis chiangdaoensis
intro: Acilepis chiangdaoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and inflammation, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Acilepis chiangdaoensis include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into a decoction or an infusion, and may also be used in combination with other herbs to create a tonic or a medicinal tea.
taxonomy: Acilepis chiangdaoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to Thailand. It belongs to the genus Acilepis, which is a group of plants closely related to the genus Leucocyclus. The plant is part of the larger clade of flowering plants, specifically within the eudicots. The classification of Acilepis chiangdaoensis is further supported by its membership in the infraclass Asteridae. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is also closely related to plants such as sunflowers and daisies.
